mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal income and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and investment demand growth, producing positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to generate positive returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.1) in quarterly regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an easing cycle reflects improving corporate output capacity and margin expansion, generating positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth during easing periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, resulting in negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative signal fails to predict downside moves, with 20-day returns remaining positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction effect vanishes when PAYEMS growth decouples from UNRATE declines, or quarterly return prediction accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations, yielding positive monthly S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with subsequent 20-day equity returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks and earnings downgrades, triggering negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Weekly returns remain positive in > 60% of severe contraction events, or signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.
